response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically capable senior individual contributor with a broad skillset spanning web, embedded, and developer tooling domains, but this role is fundamentally a people-leadership position and his resume shows no evidence of direct people management at any point in a 15+ year career. The minimum requirement of 2 years in people management is unmet, as is the preferred 3 years of leading organizational change. While he demonstrates some developer tooling exposure through CI/CD work at Altom and open-source testing libraries, this does not substitute for the core managerial competencies — performance management, team development, and strategic roadmap ownership — that the role demands.
strengths: Developer tooling experience across CI/CD infrastructure, test runners (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), and MCP framework development at re:cinq
Knowledge-sharing culture evident through organizing recurring Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, and delivering multi-day workshops on testing and web internals
Strong technical breadth across systems (embedded, mobile, backend, infrastructure) relevant to supporting a diverse engineering productivity team
concerns: Zero demonstrated people management experience — no direct reports, no performance reviews, no hiring or team-building mentioned across the entire resume
Purely individual contributor career trajectory with no evidence of organizational leadership, change management, or cross-team alignment ownership
No mobile platform depth at scale; iOS experience is limited to a single app maintenance handoff, which falls well short of the mobile-platform ecosystem focus of the role
